corrections.text: In the SK hynix paragraph, the article quotes the iHBM architecture as using "cooling elements made of electrically non-conductive, thermally conductive silicon" that "cuts thermal resistance by more than 30% against current products." The cited Tom's Hardware report describes "non-conductive silicon cooling elements" in the D2D PHY layer and states the reduction is "by 30%" (the report's headline and URL both say "by 30%," not "more than 30%"). The roughly 30% figure is accurate; the quoted wording added qualifiers and inflated the figure.
